title: Procurement Supplier Risk
rawSituation: Our sole supplier for a critical component has issued a force majeure notice. They cannot guarantee delivery for 12 weeks. We have 4 weeks of inventory. Our customers have firm orders. Switching supplier requires 8 weeks of qualification. The CFO says we cannot absorb the penalty for late delivery.

kernelResult.situationSummary: This is a technology or dependency situation.
kernelResult.kernelInterpretation: The system interprets this situation as a technology or dependency decision where system reliability, migration, or technical debt is the binding constraint. Dimensions surfaced: consequence.

alternativeClasses.reason: Timing pressure suggests operational execution dimension

constraintGraph.description: Sole supplier dependency — no alternative source for critical component or service
Supplier has issued force majeure or cannot guarantee delivery — supply is interrupted
Switching to alternative supplier requires significant lead time

evidenceState.label: Evidence Gap: Low documentation
evidenceState.summary: Situation relies primarily on user-reported information without independent documentation

evidenceState.label: Constraint: Sole supplier dependency
evidenceState.summary: The organisation depends on a single supplier with no qualified alternative. This creates critical vulnerability.

evidenceState.label: Constraint: Supply interruption
evidenceState.summary: Supplier cannot guarantee delivery. Supply is interrupted with no confirmed resolution timeline.

evidenceState.label: Constraint: Switching lead time
evidenceState.summary: Qualifying and switching to an alternative supplier requires significant time that may exceed available inventory.

evidenceState.label: Forbidden: Assume supply will resume without confirmed commitment
evidenceState.summary: Do not assume the supplier will resume delivery without a confirmed written commitment and recovery timeline.

adversarialChallenges.contradiction: Supply interruption creates inability to meet customer obligations, with penalty exposure
adversarialChallenges.outputEffect: Output blocked until contradiction is resolved by human review.

loadBearingAssumptions.assumption: Sole supplier dependency — no alternative source for critical component or service
loadBearingAssumptions.ifWrong: The minimum viable path may change.

loadBearingAssumptions.assumption: Supplier has issued force majeure or cannot guarantee delivery — supply is interrupted
loadBearingAssumptions.ifWrong: The minimum viable path may change.

classificationConfidence.implication: If classification is wrong, the entire lens selection and output structure changes.

informationGaps.impact: Cannot determine who holds decision mandate
informationGaps.acquisitionPath: Ask: Who has the formal authority to make this decision?

informationGaps.impact: Cannot determine what must be performed
informationGaps.acquisitionPath: Ask: What contractual, regulatory, or fiduciary obligations apply?

kernelLimitations: This analysis is based on the information provided and available evidence.
The system cannot verify independently reported facts.
Human review is recommended for high-consequence decisions.
This does not constitute legal, tax, or regulated professional advice.

minimumViablePath.description: Address the primary identified risk: Supply interruption creates inability to meet customer obligations, with penalty exposure. This is the failure point most likely to undermine the decision.
minimumViablePath.rationale: The adversarial challenge (supply-failure-vs-customer-obligation) must be resolved or consciously accepted before the decision proceeds.

minimumViablePath.description: Identify and confirm who holds formal decision mandate.
minimumViablePath.rationale: Without confirmed authority, the decision is vulnerable to challenge.

minimumViablePath.description: Verify: Sole supplier dependency — no alternative source for critical component or service
minimumViablePath.rationale: Critical constraints change what is feasible.

forbiddenActions.action: Assume customer obligations can be met without a confirmed supply recovery timeline or alternative sourcing plan
forbiddenActions.reason: Supply interruption combined with customer penalty exposure creates compound risk. A confirmed recovery plan must be in place before customer commitments are reaffirmed.

forbiddenActions.action: Do not assume the supplier will resume delivery without a confirmed written commitment and recovery timeline.
forbiddenActions.reason: Forbidden: Assume supply will resume without confirmed commitment

whatMustNotBeDelayed: Address the primary identified risk: Supply interruption creates inability to meet customer obligations, with penalty exposure. This is the failure point most likely to undermine the decision.
Identify and confirm who holds formal decision mandate.
Verify: Sole supplier dependency — no alternative source for critical component or service
